log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a UPX-packed PE executable with tags indicating persistence and runtime modules. Now I need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of indicators availab

I have a good overview of the IOC types. Now I need to dive deeper into the specific details. I'll start by getting the network activity to understand the C2 infrastructure, then examine the dropped f

The network activity shows C2 communication with bublikiadministrator.com (193.166.255.171) and attempts to resolve other domains. The HTTP request includes parameters that could be used for command-a

The dropped files show multiple malicious components: cftmon.exe (the main dropper), spools.exe (dropped to system32/drivers - likely a driver dropper), and ftp33.dll (dropped to user profile and syst

The registry changes reveal multiple persistence mechanisms: Run keys for cftmon.exe and spools.exe, Winlogon modifications (Shell, UIHost, UserInit), and a Service driver installation (Schedule). The
I have gathered comprehensive evidence about this malware. The analysis shows: 1) A UPX-packed dropper (cftmon.exe) that downloads and executes additional payloads. 2) Multiple persistence mechanisms
